title: Azulejo Estrela
description: Classic Portuguese star tile hand-painted with deep cobalt blue on a cream base. Inspired by 18th-century Lisbon palace facades, each piece is individually fired in a wood kiln, producing subtle glaze variations that make every tile unique.

title: Marrakech Grid
description: Geometric encaustic cement tile inspired by the zellige tradition of Moroccan riads. The rich earth tones and sharp star-polygon pattern make it equally striking as a floor accent or a full-wall installation.

title: Terra Umbra
description: Hand-pressed terracotta hexagon with a raw, unglazed finish that reveals natural clay variation across each batch. Ideal for rustic kitchen floors, outdoor terraces, and Mediterranean-inspired interiors.

title: Byzantine Mosaic
description: Intricate mosaic panel assembled from hand-cut glass and natural stone tesserae, reminiscent of 6th-century Byzantine church interiors. Each panel is set in white mortar and sealed for indoor or covered outdoor use.

title: Rose Garden
description: Delicate Victorian-era floral relief tile with a soft blush pink glaze and embossed rose motif. Originally designed for fireplace surrounds, it adapts beautifully to bathroom splashbacks and decorative borders.

title: Cement Lace
description: A modern reinterpretation of traditional lace motifs pressed into encaustic cement. The crisp white pigment and negative-space pattern create an airy, textile-like quality perfect for Scandinavian or French minimalist interiors.

title: Indigo Wave
description: Japanese-inspired wave pattern in deep navy and white porcelain, referencing Katsushika Hokusai's iconic woodblock prints. Double-fired for exceptional hardness and suitable for wet areas including shower walls.

title: Andalusian Star
description: Eight-pointed star tile in burnt sienna and ivory, a hallmark of Moorish geometric art found throughout the palaces of Seville and Granada. Machine-pressed for consistent sizing with a hand-applied oxide glaze.

title: Sage Subway
description: Elongated subway tile in a muted sage green with a crackled glaze finish. The slight surface crackle is an intentional design choice that adds depth and a handcrafted warmth rarely found in standard metro tiles.

title: Oaxacan Barro
description: Hand-modelled black clay tile from the Zapotec tradition of Oaxaca, Mexico. The lustrous surface is achieved without any glaze — purely through burnishing and a low-smoke firing technique passed down over generations.

title: Art Deco Fan
description: Fan-shaped relief tile in matte gold and midnight teal, referencing the geometric glamour of 1920s Parisian interiors. Arrange in a staggered row to create a sweeping scallop wall effect.

title: Nordic Frost
description: Minimalist large-format porcelain slab with a soft white-grey gradient that mimics natural quartzite. Low water absorption and high slip resistance make it suitable for both residential and light commercial flooring.
